title: 10 best herbal teas for cough
intro_desc: Herbal teas for cough are infusions made from various plants, flowers, and spices, which can help alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds. These teas often contain natural anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties that can soothe irritated throats, reduce inflammation, and help expel mucus. Examples of herbal teas that help with cough include thyme, licorice root, slippery elm, and eucalyptus. By drinking these teas, individuals can find relief from coughing fits, leading to improved sleep, increased productivity, and an overall better quality of life. For instance, a study published in the Journal of Ethnopharmacology found that thyme tea was as effective as a common cough syrup in relieving acute bronchitis symptoms, highlighting the potential benefits of herbal teas for cough.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena tea helps with cough because it contains antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the throat and help alleviate irritation, making it easier to cough up mucus and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract. Additionally, lemon verbena is rich in antioxidants that boost the immune system, helping the body fight off infections and speed up recovery. Furthermore, the tea's soothing and calming effects can help reduce stress and promote better sleep, which is essential for a quicker recovery from a cough.

Marshmallow tea helps with cough because it contains mucilage, a substance that becomes slippery when it comes into contact with water. This mucilage coats the throat and soothes irritation, reducing the urge to cough. Additionally, it is believed to have anti-tussive and expectorant properties, which help to expel mucus from the respiratory tract and alleviate congestion. Furthermore, marshmallow tea has anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce inflammation in the airways and ease breathing. Its soothing and calming effects make it an ideal natural remedy for coughs and sore throats.

Daisy tea helps with cough because it contains antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the throat and reduce irritation. The herbal tea's natural healing compounds, such as flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, work together to alleviate coughing and ease congestion. Additionally, daisy tea has expectorant properties that help clear phlegm and mucus from the respiratory tract, providing relief from persistent coughs and making breathing easier. Its soothing and calming effects on the nervous system also help reduce coughing fits, promoting relaxation and restful sleep.

Pot marigold tea helps with cough because it contains anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that aid in soothing a sore throat and expelling mucus from the respiratory tract. The tea's warm temperature also helps to alleviate congestion and reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, pot marigold tea is rich in antioxidants that boost the immune system, helping the body to fight off infections that may cause coughing. Drinking pot marigold tea can provide relief from cough symptoms and promote overall respiratory health.

Cannabis tea helps with cough because it contains cannabinoids, such as THC and CBD, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. These compounds can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, relieving cough and irritation. Additionally, cannabis tea can help relax the muscles in the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the frequency and severity of coughing fits. The soothing, warm nature of the tea can also help to calm a sore throat and alleviate symptoms of congestion. Furthermore, cannabis tea can help stimulate the release of mucus, making it easier to expel and clear the airways.

Bell pepper tea helps with cough because it contains a rich amount of vitamin C and antioxidants that work to soothe the throat, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. The capsaicin in bell peppers acts as a natural decongestant, helping to clear mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract. Additionally, the tea's warm temperature can provide relief by alleviating congestion and providing comfort to a sore throat. By drinking bell pepper tea, individuals can find relief from their cough and promote a faster recovery.

Cinnamon tea helps with cough because it has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that soothe the throat and help clear mucus. The spicy flavor of cinnamon stimulates the production of saliva, which keeps the throat lubricated and reduces irritation. Additionally, cinnamon is rich in antioxidants that boost the immune system and help the body fight off infections that can cause coughing. The warm temperature of the tea also helps to relax the muscles in the chest and alleviate coughing spasms. Drinking cinnamon tea can provide quick relief from a persistent cough and promote overall respiratory health.

Siberian ginseng tea helps with cough because it contains active compounds called eleutherosides, which have been shown to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. This herbal tea has expectorant properties that help to loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, providing relief from a persistent cough. Additionally, Siberian ginseng tea is known to soothe throat irritation, thereby alleviating the discomfort associated with coughing. Its antiviral and antibacterial properties also help to fight off infections that may cause cough, making it an effective natural remedy for cough relief.

Tasmanian blue gum tea helps with cough because it contains anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The tea is made from the leaves of the Tasmanian blue gum tree, which is native to Australia. The leaves contain compounds called eucalyptus oil and alpha-pinene, which help to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and thin mucus, making it easier to expel. Additionally, the tea's soothing warmth can help to alleviate irritation in the throat, providing relief from persistent coughing. Drinking Tasmanian blue gum tea can help to reduce the severity and duration of coughs, making it a natural and effective remedy for respiratory ailments.

Eastern purple coneflower tea helps with cough because it contains antitussive and expectorant properties that soothe irritated throats and reduce coughing. This herbal remedy, also known as Echinacea, has been used in traditional medicine to alleviate respiratory symptoms. Its active compounds, such as alkamides and caffeic acid, work together to stimulate the immune system, reduce inflammation, and clear mucus from the airways. As a result, drinking eastern purple coneflower tea can provide relief from both acute and chronic coughs, providing a natural and effective alternative to over-the-counter cough suppressants.
